---
title: "PlanForge DAGs and Intelligence Arbitrage"
chapter_id: "planforge-dags-and-intelligence-arbitrage"
part_id: "planning-memory-reasoning-execution"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "planforge"
  - "planforge_compiler_arch"
  - "coherence_exchange"
  - "cognitive_compilation"
  - "tokenmana"
  - "moecot"
evidence_level: "argument"
claim_label: "Design rationale"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# PlanForge DAGs and Intelligence Arbitrage

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `planforge-dags-and-intelligence-arbitrage` |
| Part | Part II - Planning, Memory, Reasoning, and Execution |
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `planforge`, `planforge_compiler_arch`, `coherence_exchange`, `cognitive_compilation`, `tokenmana`, `moecot` |
| Claim label | Design rationale |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from `book_structure.json` and the source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

Large tasks require decomposition, scheduling, tiered model selection, and cost-aware routing.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

Uniformly applying the strongest model to every task wastes compute and can obscure which tasks require verification or escalation.

## Core Claim

[planforge-dags-and-intelligence-arbitrage.core, label: Design rationale, support: argument] PlanForge-style DAG planning can support intelligence arbitrage by assigning each node the minimum adequate capability and verification burden.

## Mechanism

- Decompose goals into DAGs.
- Annotate nodes with capability tier, budget, context request, risk, and verification requirement.
- Merge redundant branches and escalate only when predicates fail.

## Interfaces

- Routing consumes capability annotations.
- VCM consumes context requests.
- Evidence records cost/quality results.

## Invariants

- Dependency order is respected.
- Cost savings do not remove required verification.
- Escalation paths are explicit.

## Failure modes

- Wrong tier selection.
- Dependency cycles.
- Economic optimization overriding safety.

## Minimal implementation

A DAG schema with node-level capability and verification annotations.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| DAG acyclicity test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Capability tier assignment test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Escalation trigger test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `planforge` | PlanForge | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Planning substrate. Goal-to-execution compilation, hierarchical decomposition, DAG planning, scheduling, intelligence arbitrage. |
| `planforge_compiler_arch` | PlanForge: A Compiler Architecture for AI Task Orchestration | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Later/alternate PlanForge framing. Prefer highest-quality/latest content after comparison. |
| `coherence_exchange` | The Coherence Exchange | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Found in AI generated paper dump. Use carefully; speculative synthesis of PlanForge, Spinoza, Talos, UAT, Alignment Field. |
| `cognitive_compilation` | Cognitive Compilation | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Compiler framing for LLM-centered planning, semantic IR, target compilation, incremental repair. |
| `tokenmana` | TokenMana | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Regenerative capacity mechanisms for load-stable token pricing. |
| `moecot` | MoECOT-Agent Architecture Whitepaper | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Concrete implementation evidence: governed low-parameter multi-core runtime, readiness gates, ledgers, replay. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
